mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 195, equity discount rates rise and real purchasing power deteriorates. This dual monetary tightening signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=3.5% and CPIAUCSL=196.1 approaching trigger threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress and recession risk rises.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following PAYEMS declines are positive in more than 50% of instances, or rolling 3-year Rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1900 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously decline, aggregate demand weakness emerges. This interaction signals reduced future corporate revenues and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 120 index points sustained over 3 months indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand and corporate earnings surprise to the upside.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods of INDPRO > 120 are negative in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.2% in a single month, it signals unexpected labor market deterioration and triggers flight-to-safety behavior. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
regime_hint: labor market shock or cyclical downturn
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust aggregate demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate revenue growth accelerates and valuation multiples re-rate upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
regime_hint: above-trend expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) falls below 3.0%, monetary accommodation expands and equity discount rates compress. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield and growth exposure.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or crisis response
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 118 index points while unemployment (UNRATE) simultaneously rises above 5.0%, signaling stagflationary supply shock. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress and growth expectations deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO=119.678 and UNRATE=4.9% near thresholds
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
